A 27-year-old male working at RIG, Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates, returned to Kozhikode on 12.11.2025. He presented with complaints ofmultiple pustular lesions over thegenitalia and pubic region on 16.11.2025; followed by fever whichstarted on 18.11.2025. Initially, heconsulted in a private clinic at Kakkodion 18.11.2025 and again on 20.11.2025. From there he was referredto MCH KKD for further managementand consulted at the MCH Dermatologydepartment on 21.11.2025. Samples (Lesion swab, TS, Swab from crust, urine) were collected. First sample ofthe patient tested positive for Mpox by RTPCR on 22.11.2025 at MCH KKD. The second sample tested positive for Mpox (Clade I) on 26.11.2025 at NIV Pune. Subsequently, third, fourth andfifth sample collected on 29.11.2025, 08.12.2025 and 12.12.2025 testedpositive for Mpox (RTPCR) at NIV Pune. The patient tested negative for Mpox on 19.12.2025 at NIV Pune. The second case, a 27-year-old female, primary contact of the first case, presented with fever, sore throat andlymph node enlargement which startedon 25.11.2025. The patient consulted at MCH KKD on 26.11.2025 wheremedicines were given. Due to persistentthroat pain and a single papule overright ring finger palmar aspect, sheagain consulted at MCH KKD on 28.11.2025 and her samples were takenfor testing. The patient tested positivefor Mpox (Clade I) at NIV Pune on 29.11.2025. She was advised homeisolation. Subsequently, samplescollected on 03.12.2025, 12.12.2025 and 19.12.2025 tested positive for Mpox (RTPCR) at NIV Pune. Thepatient tested negative for Mpox on 27.12.2025 at NIV Pune. Both thepatients recovered. Contacts weretraced and isolated for 21 days. Feversurvey was done. Communityawareness and health education givenby the peripheral team on isolation ofcases, hygiene maintenance and propernutrition.

Community awareness and healtheducation given by the peripheral team onisolation of cases, hygiene maintenanceand proper nutrition.

Community awareness on safe food habits, hygiene and sanitation.

Contact tracing conducted. [LATE REPORT]

Cases reported from Village Sahupara, Ward No.22. District RRT investigated the outbreak. House to house survey done. On 28.12.18 several cases of jaundice reported in UPHC Mowa. The affected population belonged to low socio economic status. It was revealed that drinking water was contaminated with sewage. Most affected age group was 16 to 30 yrs and females were mostly affected49 Blood Samples were collected and 22 were confirmed Hepatitis E. Water Samples send to JNM Medical College for Bacterial Culture Test, water sample result unsatisfactory for drinking. Confirmation of etiology and tested at IDSP referral lab Govt. Medical College, Raipur. Survey in all affected area done by District Combat Team. Symptomatic treatment of all cases. Communicated to Municipal Dept. & PHE Dept. for Sanitation and hygiene.

A 24yr old female from Village Chelanur, PHC Puthiyappa, Puthur, Elathur presented symptoms with fever, altered sensorium, ptosis, seizure and was admitted on 13th 01-07-18 in Govt Medical College, Kozhikode. Serum and CSF samples were sent to NIV Pune. The PCR sample of CSF and serum were negative for Dengue, Chikungunya, Zika, JE and West Nile Virus. The serology of serum sample was positive for West Nile Virus (IgM ELISA). The case had multiple episode of epilepsy and CSF showed increased protein and low sugar indicating tuberculosis meningitis. Case was treated with anti TB drug. The Zonal Entomology Team carried out vector survey; fogging activities were undertaken along with larvicidal spraying in the affected area. Integrated Vector Management activities were carried out in the affected area.

Well has been chlorinated and all cases treated at health facility.

A 23 year old male residing at Vadakkekara, Paravur a student of Al Akbar College Thodupuzha, presented symptoms of fever with altered sensorium was referred to a private hospital at Eranakulam on 30th 01-05-19. Throat swab, CSF, blood and urine were collected and sent to NIV Pune; the samples were found positive for Nipah Virus (Real-time RT-PCR, nested RT-PCR and for anti-Nipah human IgM and IgG antibodies by ELISA assay). Presently the case is stable. Central team and State/District RRT investigated the outbreak. Control room was set up at State Headquarter (Trivandrum) and District Headquarter (Ernakulum). 330 contacts were traced and 13 suspected cases samples were found negative for Nipah. 141 bats were captured and samples were collected (rectal and throat swabs) were tested. Out of these one bat sample was found positive for Nipah viral RNA. Standard NIPAH outbreak guidelines were implemented in the hospital and community settings. Health education given.

A total of 729 cases and 153 deaths due to AES were reported from Bihar till 26th 01-06-19. Further 113 cases and 15 death were reported till 4th 01-07-19. Central Team and Medical officer from ROHFW Patna are deputed at State level to support State and District Teams.

Cases of AES reported from various villages of multiple districts of Assam. State and Central level RRT investigated the outbreak.
